label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's tackle this question. The main idea of a passage is usually what the whole text is mostly about. The question is asking for the main idea of the passage, and the options are A, B, C, D. First, I'll read through the passage again to get a sense of what it's discussing. The passage starts by talking about London's subway system and its historical value, but then shifts focus. It mentions that when you ride the subway, people avoid eye contact and are busy with their own activities. Instead of reading books or thinking quietly, they use technology. The text goes on to talk about how Apple products like the iPhone and apps are popular among commuters. It lists various apps and how they're used, and also mentions iPods and even palm DVD technology for watching shows. The conclusion is that with all these distractions, people might forget to get off the train. Now, looking at the options: A) London commuters are unfriendly to strangers. The passage says they avoid eye contact, but clarifies it's not rudeness, just that they're too busy. So this is more about behavior but maybe not the main idea. B) Apple has earned a lot of money from 40,000 apps. The passage does mention Apple and the number of apps, but this is just one part of the text. The main idea isn't solely about Apple's profits. C) Technology is changing how London commuters spend their traveling time. The passage discusses how technology (iPhones, apps, iPods, DVDs) is used by commuters, replacing quiet habits. This seems to be the central theme. D) Riding a London subway is a must when visiting London. The first part mentions this, but the rest of the passage focuses more on the commuting habits and technology. So the main idea is more about the technology aspect rather than
